title: dalbergia cuscatlanica
intro: Dalbergia cuscatlanica, also known as the Cuscatlán rosewood or Cuscatlán mahogany, is a tree species native to Central America. The bark and wood of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dalbergia cuscatlanica include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and pain reliever. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia cuscatlanica typically involve the bark being dried and ground into a powder or made into a tea, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
